title: We built an AI that watches YouTube/Insta trading videos and codes the strategy for you.
author: Algonumm
selftext:
We’ve added a new feature to QntAIfy that completely eliminates the need to code. You can now build a trading strategy just by typing it out in plain English or simply by pasting a video link from YouTube, Instagram, or X.

The AI automatically analyzes the video, extracts the exact entry and exit logic, and runs a full historical backtest to give you your win rate and max drawdown. We built this to make algorithmic trading accessible to people who understand the markets but don't want to become software developers.

See a strategy you like online? Just paste the link. Zero coding. Zero effort. Let the AI build it. **Link:** Qntaify .com

comments.author: Denis_Kurilchik
comments.body:
The interesting challenge with extracting strategies from video is that most trading content is descriptive rather than systematic. someone says 'look for a break of this level then wait for a retest' - that's hard to codify into backtest rules without significant interpretation on the AI's part.

Has the system been tested on more quantitative content, like written algo trade writeups or academic research, where the logic tends to be more explicit? Curious whether the accuracy gap between structured and unstructured source material is significant.

comments.author: FortuneGrouchy4701
comments.body: Youtube videos are for dummies, can it do a simple true one like this? https://hftbacktest.readthedocs.io/en/latest/tutorials/High-Frequency%20Grid%20Trading%20-%20Simplified%20from%20GLFT.html
